name: Potato-Stuffed Enchiladas
recipeIngredient: 1 pound Russet potatoes, peeled and diced
recipeInstructions:
Servings: 4
POTATO STUFFING
Stuffing:
Heat 1 tablespoon olive oil in a heavy large non-stick skillet over high heat. Add potatoes and cook until golden, stirring often, about 8 minutes. Reduce heat to low.
Add chili powder and cumin seeds and cook for 2 minutes. Add tomatoes, sliced green onions, and tomato sauce. Cover and cook until the potatoes are tender, about 6 minutes.
Add 1/3 cup Jack cheese, egg, cilantro, and jalapeno. Stir until the cheese melts. Season with salt and cayenne pepper.
ENCHILADA SAUCE
Heat 2 tablespoons olive oil in a heavy large skillet over high heat. Add chopped onions and garlic and sauté for 5 minutes.
Mix in cumin seeds, chili powder, dried oregano, and ground cinnamon, and sauté for 1 minute.
Add enchilada sauce and tomato sauce and simmer for 2 minutes. Mix in fresh lime juice. Season to taste with salt and cayenne pepper.
Topping
Preheat the oven to 450 degrees Fahrenheit. Pour the enchilada sauce over the enchiladas. Sprinkle with feta cheese and remaining 1 1/3 cups Jack cheese. Cover with foil. Bake until heated through and bubbling, about 30 minutes. Serve with sour cream and green onions.
